evals.name: new-integration-email
evals.prompt: I'm working in the repo under inputs/eval-0-new-integration-email/ (Python, organized as app/ infra/ run/). When a user signs up we now need to send them a welcome email through SendGrid. Where should the email-sending code go, and how do I wire it in? Walk me through the placement before writing any code.

assertions.text: Declares an email/notifier interface (port) in the reason — app/ — owned where it's needed
Places the SendGrid SDK implementation in the connections (infra/), not in app/
Keeps the SendGrid SDK import out of app/ — the reason takes on no outward runtime dependency on SendGrid
Wires the concrete SendGrid implementation in run/main.py (how it's run)
Has the signup use-case invoke the email step through the interface — the decision to send the welcome email lives in the reason

evals.name: wrong-way-dependency
evals.prompt: Code review on inputs/eval-1-wrong-way-dependency/internal/order/order.go (Go, layout is internal/ + cmd/). Something feels off about the Order type depending on the database directly. Is this structured right? If not, what would you change?

assertions.text: Identifies that database/sql I/O inside the domain Order type is a smell — the reason points outward at a concrete
Recommends a repository-style interface (port) owned by the domain/reason
Moves the SQL into an adapter/connection (e.g. internal/postgres), leaving the domain free of database/sql
Keeps the order-total business rule in the domain, not pushed into the adapter
Notes the construction/wiring belongs in cmd (how it's run)

evals.name: new-entrypoint-cli
evals.prompt: In inputs/eval-2-new-entrypoint-cli/ (TypeScript, src/core src/adapters src/run). We trigger a catalog reindex from an HTTP endpoint today. I want to also run the same reindex from a cron job as a CLI command, without duplicating the reindex logic. How should I add that?

assertions.text: Reuses the existing reindexCatalog use-case in core rather than duplicating its logic
Adds the new CLI as an entrypoint under run/ (how it's run)
Wires the same adapters (ProductStore, SearchIndex) into the CLI entrypoint
Keeps the CLI entrypoint thin — no business logic added to run/
Does not introduce a new outward dependency from core to make this work
